free_text: Studies of (d,alpha) and (d,d) reactions on 32S
2.5 MeV electrostatic accelerator.
Two silicon semiconductor detectors placed
inside the scattering chamber. One of the detectors
at 135 degree with respect to beam was used as monitor.
A layer of Ag2S evaporated on a thin silver backing.
The thickness of the target was about 0.30 mg/cm2,
which correspond to the energy loss 30 keV for 2.0 MeV
deuterons.
The deuteron beam intensity on the target was also
measured by the current integrator.
No details on uncertainties given.
VS
Form the text of the article: The intensity of the
alpha1-group leading to the excited state 0.684 MeV
is very small since this transition is forbidden by
the conservation law of the isospin. Thus the
intensity of the alpha1,2 peak can be considered to
be practically due to the alpha2-group leading to
0.705 MeV excitation energy.
Scanned from Fig.2 of NC,38,52,1965.
